posts.title: Low block 5-4-1 — away games vs stronger teams
posts.body:
## Shape

**5-4-1** — five defenders, four midfielders, one striker holding the ball up.

## When to use

Away games against top-3 teams. Also useful when we are protecting a lead late in the game.

## Key principles

1. **Stay compact** — no more than 25m between defensive and midfield lines
2. **No individual pressing** — press only as a unit on a trigger
3. **Win the ball → transition fast** — long ball to the striker's run immediately
4. **Second balls** — midfield must be aggressive winning loose balls in front of the block

## Triggers to press out of the low block

- Their CB plays backwards to the GK
- Their full-back receives with no pressure and head is down

posts.title: High press 4-3-3
posts.body:
## Shape

**4-3-3** — three forwards press in pairs from the front.

## Pressing triggers

- GK pass to CB → two forwards engage immediately
- Slow build-up → all three forwards press
- Their pivot receives and turns → nearest midfielder closes

## What the rest of the team does

Midfield shifts towards the ball to cut passing lanes. The two wide midfielders tuck in. Defenders push up to compress space and stay close to the midfield line.

## If the press is broken

Reset immediately into a 4-4-2 mid-block. Don't chase the ball — get organised.
